stem: For how many integers \( x \) is \( x^{2} + ([[b]])x + ([[c]]) < 0 \)?
solution: The quadratic factors as \( (x - ([[p]]))(x - ([[q]])) \), which is negative exactly on the open interval \( ([[p]], [[q]]) \). The integers strictly between [[p]] and [[q]] number \( [[q]] - ([[p]]) - 1 = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the length of the interval, but counting integers strictly inside requires subtracting 1.
This counts as if both endpoints were included, but the inequality is strict.
This excludes one integer too many.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.

stem: For how many integers \( x \) is \( \dfrac{x - ([[p]])}{x - ([[q]])} \le 0 \)?
solution: The quotient is zero at \( x = [[p]] \) (included, since the numerator vanishing satisfies \( \le 0 \)) and undefined at \( x = [[q]] \) (must be excluded). Between them the numerator and denominator have opposite signs, so the quotient is negative. The solution set is the half-open interval \( [[[p]], [[q]]) \), which contains \( [[q]] - ([[p]]) = [[answer]] \) integers.
distractor_rationales: This treats \( [[q]] \) as included, but the expression is undefined there and must be excluded.
This also excludes \( [[p]] \), but the numerator vanishing there is allowed by \( \le 0 \).
This treats both endpoints as included, ignoring that the denominator's zero must be excluded.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is smaller than the correct count.

stem: For how many integers \( x \) with \( [[p]] \le x \le [[r]] \) is \( (x - ([[p]]))(x - ([[q]]))(x - ([[r]])) \le 0 \)?
solution: On \( [[[p]], [[r]]] \), a sign chart of the three factors shows the product is positive on \( ([[p]], [[q]]) \) and non-positive everywhere else in the window (zero at each root, negative on \( ([[q]], [[r]]) \)). So the satisfying integers are \( x = [[p]] \) together with all integers from \( [[q]] \) to \( [[r]] \): a total of \( 1 + ([[r]] - [[q]] + 1) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to include the isolated point \( x = [[p]] \), where the product is exactly zero.
This uses the wrong reference root, spanning from \( [[p]] \) instead of from \( [[q]] \).
This omits one endpoint of the qualifying range \( [[[q]], [[r]]] \).
This is larger than the correct count.
This is smaller than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.

stem: For how many integers \( x \) with \( [[L]] \le x \le [[U]] \) is \( (x - ([[p]]))(x - ([[q]])) > 0 \)?
solution: The product is non-positive exactly on the closed interval \( [[[p]], [[q]]] \) (zero at the roots, negative between them), which contains \( [[q]] - ([[p]]) + 1 \) integers. Removing these from all \( [[U]] - ([[L]]) + 1 \) integers in the window \( [[[L]], [[U]]] \) leaves the integers where the product is strictly positive: \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This removes only the OPEN interior count instead of the closed interval (the roots themselves must also be excluded from the answer).
This reports the size of the excluded closed interval instead of its complement in the window.
This reports the size of the whole window without removing the excluded interval at all.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is smaller than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.
